channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: These tech leaders have jobs in the Trump administration
item.description: President Trump has been tapping a number of tech executives for a range of roles in his second administration over the past few months. Now, the first week of Trump’s second term, his cabinet is powering through confirmation hearings and unofficial advisors are assuming their posts.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Trump pardons founder of Silk Road website
item.description: President Donald Trump has pardoned Ross Ulbricht, the founder of Silk Road, an underground website for selling drugs.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Facebook, Instagram Users Followed by Newly Inaugurated President Trump
item.description: Some Facebook and Instagram users found themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance after inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk’s straight-arm gesture embraced by right-wing extremists regardless of what he meant
item.description: The confusion hasn’t stopped critics and fans alike of the world’s richest man from reading into the gesture what they wanted.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: ServiceOps: Unleashing a new AI agent to reduce change failures in complex systems
item.description: Agentic AI promises to transform enterprise IT work. For CIOs and IT leaders, this means improved operational efficiency, data-driven decision making and accelerated innovation.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Ensuring SMBs don’t get left behind in the Gen AI wave
item.description: Don't let your SMB get left behind! Harness AI's power with expert guidance and the right tools for transformative growth.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Nintendo Switch 2 backwards compatibility is ‘the best direction to take’ for players, Nintendo says
item.description: Backwards compatibility on the Nintendo Switch 2 was the “best decision” for all consumers, according to Nintendo.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Where CIOs should place their 2025 AI bets
item.description: We may look back at 2024 as the year when LLMs became mainstream, every enterprise SaaS added copilot or virtual assistant capabilities, and many organizations got their first taste of agentic AI.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Delays in TSMC’s Arizona plant spark supply chain worries
item.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) has said it is unlikely to equip its new US plant in Arizona with its most advanced chip technology ahead of its Taiwan factories, raising concerns about supply-chain hurdles for tech companies.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump, a populist president, is flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Some of the most exclusive seats at President Donald Trump’s inauguration on Monday were reserved for powerful tech CEOs who also happen to be among the world’s richest men.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump orders government not to infringe on Americans’ speech, calls for censorship investigation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday ordered that no federal officer, employee or agent may unconstitutionally abridge the free speech of any American citizen, an early step toward his campaign promise to dismantle what he called government “censorship” of U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: For TikTok users, mourning, frustration and clinging to hope as TikTok ban looms
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. is inching closer and closer to a potential TikTok ban — with the nation's highest court upholding a law that's set to halt new downloads of the app starting Sunday.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and MAE ANDERSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Who could buy TikTok? Only a few parties are serious about offering
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The supremely popular TikTok could be banned on Jan. 19 under a federal law that forces the video sharing platform to divest itself from its China-based parent company, ByteDance, or shut down its U.S. operations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Federated learning: The killer use case for generative AI
item.description: Let’s imagine a fictional company, Global Retail Corporation, a multinational retail chain struggling with its initial approach to AI integration.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Biden's new executive order aims to shore up US cyber defenses
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden issued an executive order Thursday aimed at strengthening the nation's cybersecurity and making it easier to go after foreign adversaries or hacking groups that try to compromise U.S. internet and telecommunication systems.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Powerful Webb Telescope captures photos of one of the earliest supernova ever seen
item.description: The James Webb Space Telescope captured photos of one of the earliest supernovas ever seen using infrared technology, and creating a time lapse of the phenomena.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to expedite your CMMC process
item.description: The Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification rule is now final, yet we still see organizations seeking certification waiting to implement the requirements. The post How to expedite your CMMC process first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Biden's big semiconductor law will ramp up US chip production -- but at a high cost, report finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A sweeping 2022 law, touted by President Joe Biden as a way to revive U.S. manufacturing of semiconductors and reduce the country’s reliance on foreign-made computer chips, will “sharply increase production’’ of semiconductors in the United States.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
